content:
- Added a dedicated README-content contract plus separate README workspace support, rewrote the README into three operator-only quick-start sections, and removed README-only assertions from the heavy novice runtime contract.
- Verified the new content boundary with focused runner tests, then ran the required repository gates: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed; the default suite kept long-lane tests ignored as expected.
- Next: commit all tracked and new task/code changes, push, and quit immediately.

- Read the Choose-Task rules, prior progress, refreshed `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, and confirmed the actual repo gates come from a Rust `Makefile` (`cargo clippy`, `cargo test`).
- The refreshed active summary currently shows three failing non-bug tasks; no active bug task is visible in the summary so far.
- Next: inspect the active task files plus `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md` for `priority` and `blocked_by` tags, then select the highest-precedence unblocked task and write its path to `.ralph/current_task.txt`.

- Refreshed the Ralph task summary, checked the chooser rules, and confirmed the actual repo architecture is a Rust workspace (`runner`, `setup-sql`, shared support crates) plus a Go-based verify image under `cockroachdb_molt`.
- Resolved the active candidates: no active failing bug tasks are present; story 21 task 07 is explicitly suspended in its own task text; story 24 tasks 01 and 02 already pass; story 24 task 03 is the best live unblocked failing verification task because it targets the current README-driven operator CLI surface across runner/setup-sql/verify.
- Wrote `.ralph/current_task.txt` to `.ralph/tasks/story-24-readme-only-novice-e2e/03-task-verify-cli-command-complexity-stays-low-and-help-works-everywhere.md`. If resuming after a context cut, start that task with the `tdd` skill and keep an eye out for bugs that must be split out immediately.

- Read the prior Ralph progress, current task pointer, task-03 markdown, and the required `tdd` / `improve-code-boundaries` skills.
- Inspected the actual CLI surface in `crates/setup-sql`, `crates/runner`, the README quick-start commands, the verify Go `molt verify-service` commands, and the existing CLI/help contract tests.
- Found the main boundary smell for task 03: command-shape and help assertions are fragmented across setup-sql tests, runner tests, verify image tests, and Go verifyservice tests with no single README-facing owner. Next step is to add a planning artifact plus a task-file `<plan>` pointer, ending the plan with `NOW EXECUTE` so the next turn can implement via vertical TDD slices.

- Created the planning artifact for story-24 task 03 at `.ralph/tasks/story-24-readme-only-novice-e2e/03-task-verify-cli-command-complexity-stays-low-and-help-works-everywhere_plans/2026-04-20-cli-help-simplicity-plan.md` and updated the task markdown with a matching `<plan>` pointer.
- The plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`. It centers the task on one shared README-facing operator CLI contract owner, then vertical TDD slices for setup-sql, runner, verify image / verify-service help, and final boundary cleanup.
- Next turn should read the task and plan, then execute exactly from Slice 1 onward using the `tdd` mindset: add the shared operator CLI surface support, drive missing help coverage red-green, create an `add-bug` task immediately if any real CLI/help defect appears, and stop only after `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` pass.

- Resumed story-24 task 03 by loading prior progress, current task pointer, and the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions.
- Confirmed the task was already switched to execution mode in the previous turn; now reading the task markdown and its plan to execute via vertical red-green slices.
- Next: inspect the plan details, run the first failing focused tests, then implement only the minimum contract changes needed before full repo gates.

- Added the first tracer-bullet test for a shared operator CLI surface owner, verified the expected red state when `support/operator_cli_surface.rs` did not exist, then added the minimal typed support and reran to green.
- The new shared support currently centralizes the three README-facing surfaces (`setup-sql`, `runner`, `verify-service-image`) with their allowed actions and max action depth.
- Next: drive Slice 2 by extending `setup-sql` tests to consume the shared owner and fail on direct subcommand help coverage before implementing the shared assertion helpers.

- Reused the new shared operator CLI owner from both `setup-sql` and `runner` tests, replacing scattered root-help assertions with typed root/subcommand contracts and adding direct `--help` coverage for each README-used action.
- Verified focused greens for `cargo test -p setup-sql --test cli_contract` and `cargo test -p runner --test cli_contract`. The Go verifyservice help tests also run successfully once `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto` is used instead of the local pinned toolchain.
- Next: centralize verify image help assertions onto the shared operator surface contract, add missing `verify-service --help` coverage in Go, then run the required repo-wide gates.

- Centralized the README-facing CLI surface into `crates/runner/tests/support/operator_cli_surface.rs`, reused it from `setup-sql`, `runner`, and verify-image tests, and added direct Go root-help coverage for `molt verify-service --help`.
- Tightened the default test lane so `make test` now runs `go test ./cmd/verifyservice -count=1` with `GOTOOLCHAIN=auto`, which keeps the new verify-service command help contract in the standard gate without dragging in DB-backed Go packages.
- Verified the required gates are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test` all passed. Next: mark task 03 passed in the Ralph task file, run `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, commit all task/code/progress changes, push, and quit.
